channel.title: AInothing.top
channel.description: A warm editorial blog focused on real revenue breakdowns for AI and internet businesses, with supporting analysis on tools, traffic, and repeatable growth.

item.title: How to read a $9,000/month niche content site without confusing traffic for a business
item.description: Content-site revenue claims only become useful when you connect pageviews to monetization layers, audience intent, and long-term maintenance burden.

item.title: What a $30,000/month solo agency really depends on
item.description: Solo agency revenue becomes more believable when you inspect deal size, delivery constraints, referral loops, and how much founder time each client consumes.

item.title: Breaking down a $18,000/month AI template shop before calling it passive income
item.description: Digital product revenue looks passive from the outside, but the real story usually depends on audience trust, packaging, and continuous iteration.

item.title: Inside a $25,000/month micro-SaaS with a small but serious B2B niche
item.description: A micro-SaaS revenue claim becomes useful when you examine pricing power, retention, niche urgency, and the real acquisition loop.

item.title: Breaking down a $12,000/month affiliate site without romanticizing SEO
item.description: Affiliate site revenue gets interesting when you separate ranking luck from durable search demand, monetization depth, and product intent.

item.title: Breaking down a niche newsletter business beyond the top-line number
item.description: Newsletter revenue stories become useful when they separate audience trust, sponsorship economics, paid subscriptions, and distribution leverage.

item.title: How to read SaaS revenue screenshots without getting fooled
item.description: A good teardown asks what the number represents, over what time period, and what hidden costs or churn dynamics sit underneath it.

item.title: The publishing rules behind AInothing.top
item.description: The site should favor truthful reporting, reusable analysis, and a pace that can actually be sustained every week.

item.title: What useful AI tool posts do differently
item.description: The best tool reviews do not rewrite the homepage. They explain who the tool helps, what tradeoffs exist, and whether it deserves a place in a real workflow.

item.title: Don’t just publish. Design the growth paths behind your AI content site
item.description: Categories are the content foundation, but compounding traffic comes from repeatable acquisition systems and distribution loops.

item.title: How to break down revenue case studies people actually want to read
item.description: Strong money stories are not just numbers. They explain the offer, the traffic engine, the conversion path, and the limits of replication.

item.title: Why a personal AI blog still matters
item.description: When everyone can repost the news, lasting value comes from filtering, judgment, and consistent updates.
